facts.source_ref: source copy brief + Bridge.xyz public docs at https://apidocs.bridge.xyz/platform/orchestration/virtual_accounts/virtual-account

facts.value: Fiat on/off-ramp and stablecoin infrastructure company (APIs for stablecoin issuance, custody, and fiat deposits/withdrawals)

facts.claim: Bridge virtual account mechanic — how fiat deposit flow works
facts.value: Bridge virtual accounts are permanent, reusable fiat deposit addresses that convert incoming fiat into crypto and deliver it to a specified destination wallet address. Customers get local bank deposit details (account/routing numbers, IBANs, etc.) and funds are automatically converted to crypto on receipt.

facts.claim: Bridge virtual account supported deposit rails
facts.value: USD (ACH or wire — US account/routing number), EUR (SEPA — IBAN), MXN (SPEI — CLABE), BRL (PIX — BR code), GBP (FPS — UK account number), COP (Bre-B — CO account number)

facts.claim: KYC requirement for Bridge virtual accounts
facts.value: Customers must be onboarded and KYC/KYB-approved before virtual accounts can be created for them

Event types in order: funds_received (deposit matched) → funds_scheduled (ACH only, in-transit) → payment_submitted (crypto payment sent, tx hash preliminary) → payment_processed (on-chain confirmed, final state). Also: in_review (manual review), refunded, refund_in_flight, refund_failed, account_update, deactivation, activation, microdeposit (not onramped).

facts.claim: Bridge KYC/KYB mechanism for customer onboarding
facts.value: Bridge Customers API accepts KYC information directly from the integrator. Bridge monitors kyc_status and endorsement status (e.g. 'base endorsement' for US rails). Customers must be KYC/KYB-approved and have valid endorsements before virtual accounts can be created. Endorsements are rail-specific (e.g. US rails require 'base endorsement').

facts.claim: Infinex roadmap items for fiat/bank deposits
facts.value: Infinex roadmap lists 'Onramp (bank account)' and 'Virtual accounts' under the TradFi & CEX category — both are planned features (not marked Done or In Progress on the public roadmap)

facts.claim: Bridge virtual account fiat-to-crypto conversion — what enters and what exits
facts.value: Incoming fiat (e.g. USD via ACH/wire, EUR via SEPA, etc.) enters a Bridge virtual account; Bridge converts it to a specified crypto asset (e.g. USDC) and delivers it on-chain to the destination wallet address. The virtual account is permanent and reusable — the same deposit details work for repeat deposits.

facts.claim: Bridge virtual account USD rail details
facts.value: USD virtual accounts provide a US bank account number and routing number, issued in the customer's name, supporting ACH and wire transfers

facts.claim: Bridge virtual account EUR rail details
facts.value: EUR virtual accounts provide an IBAN for SEPA payments

unverifiable.claim: Bridge.xyz fiat-deposit code status in the Infinex platform on the bridge-integration branch
unverifiable.reason: The platform code search tool only has access to the main branch. No Bridge.xyz code was found on main (0 matches for 'bridge', 'virtual_account', 'fiat', 'onramp' in .ts/.tsx/.json files). The bridge-integration branch is not accessible via the grep tool. The Infinex roadmap lists 'Onramp (bank account)' and 'Virtual accounts' as planned (not Done/In Progress), consistent with the claim that code has not yet landed on main.

unverifiable.claim: Which specific fiat rails (USD ACH/wire, EUR SEPA, others) are wired/enabled in the Infinex Bridge integration
unverifiable.reason: Bridge.xyz supports USD ACH/wire, EUR SEPA IBAN, MXN SPEI, BRL PIX, GBP FPS, and COP Bre-B at the provider level. However, which subset Infinex has chosen to enable in its integration could not be verified — no platform code was found on main, no public announcement specifies the rails, and the bridge-integration branch is not accessible.

unverifiable.claim: Which stablecoin(s) fiat deposits settle into within the Infinex account (e.g. USDC, USDB, other)
unverifiable.reason: Bridge.xyz virtual accounts can be configured to convert fiat to any specified crypto asset (USDC, USDB, etc.) — the destination currency is set per virtual account by the integrator. No Infinex-specific configuration was found in accessible platform code or public announcements to confirm which asset is used.
